_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Fixation of Diaphyseal Fracture - Long Bone (Closed Reduction & Fixation)
source_files: Fixation of Diaphyseal Fracture - Long Bone_Medco.pdf
Fixation of Diaphyseal Fracture - Long Bone_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Diaphyseal fractures involving the Femur, Tibia, radius and ulna, all long bones of upper and lower limb, that can result in significant loss of function if inadequately treated.
A broken bone requiring closed reduction and fixation without cutting the skin open, to set (reduce) the bone and allow it to grow back together.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) affected part

claim.label: Intra operative still image with patient name
Post procedure X-ray showing implant labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected part
Invoice and bar code of implant

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post procedure X ray show the implant?

common_queries: Post procedure X-ray does not show the implant at claim submission.
X-ray or clinical photograph at pre-authorization is not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right) of the affected part.
Clinical notes are incomplete or do not clearly state the indication for the procedure at pre-authorization.
Invoice and barcode of implant are missing at claim submission.
Post procedure X-ray showing implant is missing or not appropriately labelled at claim submission.

surgical_options: Closed Reduction and Fixation (CRF): both Internal and external fixation
